prompts.title: UPSC Mains Essay Outline Builder
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior UPSC mentor (Vision IAS / Forum IAS instructor) with 12 years of guiding successful candidates. I will paste the essay topic. Build a 1,200-word essay outline with: (1) hook (a contemporary Indian incident or quote), (2) thesis (a clear stand), (3) introduction paragraph (90 words), (4) 5 body sections (each 250 words: argument + Indian example + counterpoint + reconciliation), (5) interdisciplinary lens (history + economics + sociology + science), (6) conclusion (Gandhi / Constitution / vision-statement closure). Constraints: align with UPSC essay paper marking; cite at least 8 Indian examples (across pre-1947 + post-1947); avoid memorised phrases; balance lyrical + analytical tone; under 1,200 words.

prompts.title: GS-1 Mains Answer Structure (15 Marker)
prompts.prompt: Act as a UPSC Mains evaluator with 9 years of marking GS-1 papers. Build the structure for a 15-mark GS-1 answer (target 250 words, 9 minutes). I will paste the question. Provide: (1) 30-second decoding of directive (analyse / discuss / examine / evaluate), (2) outline (intro 30 words + 3 body points 60 words each + conclusion 30 words), (3) 2 diagrams / maps to draw (if applicable), (4) Indian examples with dates, (5) committee / report / scheme cited (NCERT, ARC, NITI Aayog), (6) value-addition phrases for Bloom-level upgrade. Constraints: cap 250 words; align with UPSC marking convention (presentation matters); cite Constitution + landmark SC judgments where relevant; no padding.

prompts.title: GS-4 Ethics Case Study Response
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior UPSC GS-4 mentor with 10 years of ethics-paper experience. I will paste a case study (300 words, dilemma scenario). Write a 250-word answer covering: (1) stakeholders identified (3-5), (2) ethical dimensions involved (utilitarian / deontological / virtue + Indian — Gandhian, Buddhist, Aristotelian frame), (3) options considered (3 with consequences), (4) chosen course of action with reasoning, (5) implementation steps + safeguards, (6) reflection on values upheld. Constraints: align with UPSC GS-4 marking; cite real Indian incidents where applicable (e.g., Ashok Khemka, Durga Shakti Nagpal); avoid moralising; show practical wisdom not idealism.

prompts.title: Current Affairs PIB Summary
prompts.prompt: Act as a current-affairs faculty at Drishti IAS with 8 years of PIB curation. I will paste a PIB release. Write a 200-word UPSC-relevant summary covering: (1) what was announced (60 words), (2) why it matters for Indian polity / economy / governance (60 words), (3) GS paper relevance (GS-2, GS-3 syllabus mapping), (4) prelims-mock MCQ generation (1 question + 4 options with answer + explanation), (5) value-addition statement for mains answer. Constraints: align with UPSC syllabus mapping; cite related schemes / reports / committees; objective tone; flag potential controversy / debate angle for mains use.

prompts.title: Optional Subject Strategy (PSIR)
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PSIR optional teacher (Subhra Ranjan / Shubhra Ranjan style) with 11 years of guidance. Build a 6-month PSIR strategy for a candidate with 8 hours/day study time. Cover: (1) Paper 1 (Political Theory + Indian Government & Politics) coverage with NCERT + Standard books (OP Gauba, Heywood, Bhargava, Chakrabarty), (2) Paper 2 (International Relations + Indian Foreign Policy) coverage (Bajpai, Pant, Pulses, current affairs), (3) weekly schedule (lectures + reading + answer-writing + revision), (4) test series enrolment, (5) thinker-by-thinker mapping, (6) integration with GS-2, (7) value-add (UN reports, Hindu Editorial, IDSA), (8) PYQ analysis. Constraints: 6 months only; balance breadth + depth; honest about hours; flag burn-out checkpoints.

prompts.title: Prelims CSAT Reasoning Question Set
prompts.prompt: Act as a CSAT (UPSC Paper 2) faculty with 9 years of training. Generate 10 reasoning questions (mix of seating arrangement, blood relations, syllogism, logical sequence, data sufficiency) at UPSC CSAT difficulty. For each: (1) question text, (2) 4 options (a/b/c/d), (3) correct answer, (4) detailed solution (60 words), (5) shortcut / trick (40 words), (6) common-mistake flag. Constraints: align with CSAT pattern (33%-cut threshold but qualifying paper); 90-second per question target; avoid CAT-level math; embed Indian context where natural (e.g., 'Rohit, Priya, Akshay sit at...').

prompts.title: PYQ Trend Analysis (Last 10 Years)
prompts.prompt: Act as a UPSC PYQ analyst with 12 years of trend analysis. Analyse last 10 years (2015-2025) of UPSC Mains GS-2 (Polity / Governance / IR). Provide: (1) topic frequency table (Constitution, Pressure Groups, Centre-State, Statutory Bodies, IR-Neighbours, IR-Major Powers, etc.), (2) directive-word patterns, (3) emerging hot topics (DPI, federalism, judicial activism, neighbourhood, Indo-Pacific), (4) low-frequency low-priority topics, (5) inter-subject overlap (with GS-3 + Essay), (6) preparation priority matrix (high-yield vs low-yield), (7) reading list aligned to high-yield topics, (8) test-paper composition tips. Constraints: data-anchored; cite specific PYQ years; align with current UPSC syllabus; avoid speculative 'this year XYZ' predictions.

prompts.title: Personality Test Interview Prep DAF
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior interview-board mock-trainer (Samkalp / Chanakya IAS Academy). Generate 25 interview questions tailored to my UPSC DAF (which I will paste — name, education, hobbies, work experience, home state). For each question: (1) question text, (2) what the board is testing (sincerity / depth / national perspective / empathy / ethics), (3) 80-word ideal answer template, (4) common pitfall to avoid, (5) follow-up question they may ask. Cover hobbies, work experience, home-state issues, current affairs from DAF, ethics dilemmas, optional subject. Constraints: align with UPSC PT marking (200 marks); balance personal + national + global lenses; honesty over impressiveness; tone humble + confident.

prompts.title: JEE Advanced Physics Problem Set
prompts.prompt: Act as an FIITJEE / Allen Kota physics faculty with 14 years. Generate 6 JEE Advanced-level physics problems on Mechanics + Rotational Dynamics. For each: (1) problem statement (numerical / matrix-match / multi-correct), (2) detailed solution (free-body diagram description, equations, numerical), (3) common pitfalls (5 frequent mistakes), (4) similar PYQ from JEE Advanced 2018-2024, (5) key concept reinforced. Constraints: align with JEE Advanced pattern (paper-1 / paper-2); difficulty ranges to ~80 percentile in selectivity; SI units; figures described in text; avoid trivial substitution.

prompts.title: NEET Biology Question Bank
prompts.prompt: Act as a NEET Biology faculty with 11 years of guiding rank-bearers. Generate 8 NEET-pattern biology MCQs covering Genetics + Molecular Biology (NCERT Class 12 chapters 5, 6). For each: (1) question stem, (2) 4 options with single correct, (3) detailed explanation (90 words), (4) NCERT page reference (per latest edition), (5) common-trap option flagged, (6) related PYQ (NEET 2020-2024). Constraints: align with NEET 2025 syllabus (NTA latest); 60-second target; NCERT verbatim where standard; avoid CBSE board-only questions; bilingual-friendly (Hindi + English term familiarity).

prompts.title: SSC CGL Quantitative Aptitude Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as an SSC CGL faculty with 10 years (Adda247 / Mahendras instructor). Build a 60-day SSC CGL Tier-1 + Tier-2 quant prep plan. Cover: (1) topic distribution per CGL syllabus (Number Systems, Algebra, Geometry, Mensuration, Trigonometry, DI), (2) daily 2-hour breakdown (60 min concept + 40 min practice + 20 min revision), (3) book + PDF resources (Rakesh Yadav / Abhinay Sharma / KD Campus), (4) test series cadence (sectional + full-length), (5) shortcut compilation (Vedic Math, approximation), (6) PYQ-bank (last 5 years), (7) speed-vs-accuracy progression milestones, (8) Tier-2 SSC AAO + Statistical Investigator orientation. Constraints: respect SSC pattern + tier-2 difficulty jump; aim 95%+ accuracy and 85% attempt; English+Hindi medium support.

prompts.title: IBPS PO Banking Awareness Notes
prompts.prompt: Act as an IBPS PO faculty (Career Power / Adda247) with 8 years. Compile a 1,200-word banking awareness note for IBPS PO Mains GA section. Cover: (1) Indian banking structure (RBI, scheduled, non-scheduled, payments banks, SFBs, regional rural), (2) RBI functions + monetary policy tools, (3) types of accounts + KYC, (4) priority sector lending norms, (5) Basel III + capital adequacy, (6) NPA + IBC framework, (7) digital banking + UPI + CBDC pilot, (8) latest RBI circulars (last 6 months), (9) banking sector schemes (PMJDY, Mudra, PMSBY, PMJJBY, APY), (10) 12 PYQ-style MCQs at end. Constraints: align with IBPS PO Mains GA section weight; INR figures with crore/lakh; cite RBI / SEBI / IRDAI; current as of 2026 cycle.

prompts.title: Daily Newspaper Reading Routine
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior UPSC mentor with a focus on newspaper-reading discipline. Build a 75-minute daily newspaper-reading routine combining The Hindu + Indian Express + PIB + RSTV / Sansad TV summaries. Cover: (1) sequence + time allocation (Hindu front + edit 30 min, Express opinion 15 min, PIB headlines 10 min, monthly current-affairs magazine cross-ref 20 min), (2) note-making method (1-pager per topic + Notion / Evernote tagging), (3) 'must-read' columnist list (P. Sainath, Gopalkrishna Gandhi, Yamini Aiyar, Pratap Bhanu Mehta, MK Venu), (4) skip-list (gossip, sports, advert), (5) revision cadence (weekly + monthly), (6) integration with GS papers, (7) test discipline (weekly current-affairs MCQ on Test). Constraints: under 75 min/day; avoid information overload; emphasise editorial reasoning over factoid memorisation.

prompts.title: Sociology Optional Answer Booster
prompts.prompt: Act as a Sociology Optional teacher (Triumph IAS / Vikash Ranjan) with 10 years. I will paste a question. Build a 250-word answer integrating: (1) a thinker (Durkheim / Weber / Marx / Parsons / Merton or Indian — Srinivas, Beteille, Dumont, Yogendra Singh), (2) a relevant Indian empirical example, (3) a current development, (4) interlinks with another sociologist, (5) critique with counter-thinker. Provide also: (a) intro hook, (b) value-addition concepts to sprinkle, (c) diagram if applicable. Constraints: align with UPSC Soc Optional Paper 1 + 2 syllabus; emphasise Indian sociology deeply; avoid westernised lens-only answers; cite specific village / urban studies.

prompts.title: Mock Interview Question Bank
prompts.prompt: Act as a UPSC interview chairman / member with 15 years on PT board. Generate 30 deeper-level interview questions across: (1) home-state controversies (3 from your state — handle dispassionately), (2) optional-subject application questions (5 — apply Sociology / PSIR / Geography to a real-world Indian issue), (3) hobby integration (4 — convert hobby to leadership / national lens), (4) ethics dilemmas (5 — bureaucrat in tough spot), (5) work experience (5 — what learnt + applied to civil service), (6) current affairs (8 — analytical, not factual). For each: 80-word ideal-answer outline + pitfall. Constraints: align with UPSC PT board style; favour candor over impression-management; flag where 'I don't know' is acceptable.

prompts.title: Time-Table for 90-Day Final Push
prompts.prompt: Act as a top-rank UPSC topper-mentor. Build a 90-day study plan for an aspirant with Mains exam in 90 days who has covered 70% syllabus once. Cover: (1) week-by-week revision (GS-1 / GS-2 / GS-3 / GS-4 / Essay / Optional), (2) test-series enrollment (GS + optional + essay), (3) answer-writing target (4 daily, 6 hours), (4) current affairs (1 hour daily, monthly compilation), (5) revision-only days (3-4 weeks before exam), (6) sleep + exercise + family time block, (7) burnout management, (8) D-1 strategy, (9) D-day mental prep. Constraints: 12 hours/day study realistic; respect health; not all-out (sleep + breaks); 1 day off per week; family / life balance.

prompts.title: Group Discussion (GD) Round Prep
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PSU / banking GD evaluator with 9 years (TCS, Infosys, SBI). Generate 12 GD topics (mix of abstract, factual, case-based, current-affairs) likely in IBPS PO / SBI PO / SSC interview / corporate IT. For each: (1) topic, (2) 8-minute structured arguments (3 for + 3 against), (3) Indian-context examples, (4) statistical anchors (NSO / RBI / Census), (5) opening + closing line, (6) common pitfalls (interrupting, emotional, deviating). Constraints: respect 12-minute GD limit; foster collaborative + critical thinking; avoid politically partisan stances; English with Hindi-loanword fluency.

prompts.title: Answer-Writing Self-Evaluation Rubric
prompts.prompt: Act as a UPSC Mains evaluator with 7 years of marking. Build a self-evaluation rubric I can apply after each Mains practice answer (250 words / 15 marks). Rubric covers: (1) directive-word adherence (analyse vs discuss vs evaluate — 2 marks), (2) introduction quality (2 marks), (3) body structure + sub-headings (3 marks), (4) examples (Indian + recent + diverse — 3 marks), (5) value-addition (committee report / NITI / SC judgment — 2 marks), (6) conclusion (forward-looking — 2 marks), (7) presentation + clarity (1 mark). Total /15. Plus: (a) common-pitfall checklist, (b) 'cut-here-if-too-long' prioritisation, (c) 5 self-improvement goals per round. Constraints: realistic UPSC marking norms; encourage iterative improvement; daily practice habit; align with toppers' answer-paper philosophy.
